title: Anatomical ID Schema
description: Defines the shape of nodes and typed edges in the anatomy DAG. Data is split across files (nodes.json, relations.json) for editor ergonomics; each file MUST carry version and conforms to this single schema, populating only the field(s) relevant to it (nodes-only, edges-only, or both). The top-level shape requires version; nodes and edges are each optional but must validate when present. Synonyms are not a separate top-level field — vernacular and alternate labels live inside node.labels[] with source='vernacular' (see ADR 0008 / Architect state P1.09). Owned by Anatomy Domain. Authority: Architect. See ADR 0001, ADR 0004.

primary_id.description: Primary node identifier. UBERON preferred (UBERON:NNNNNNN, 7 digits). FMA accepted only when no UBERON equivalent exists and a crosswalk task is on file. BODY:NNNN reserved for structures with no upstream ontology equivalent. See ADR 0004.

fma_id.description: Foundational Model of Anatomy identifier; format 'FMA:NNNN'. Used in aliases per ADR 0004.

text.description: Human-readable label in the indicated language.

script.description: Optional script tag for non-Latin scripts.

source.description: Authority for this label. Known values: 'TA2' (Terminologia Anatomica), 'UBERON', 'FMA', 'vernacular' (informal English / lay terms — replaces the retired synonyms.json file per ADR 0008).

relation_type.description: Initial relation enum. Additions require an ADR per ADR 0001.

labels.description: Canonical names in one or more languages. First entry is the preferred display. TA2 is the preferred source per ADR 0004. Vernacular / lay labels are mixed in here with source='vernacular'.

aliases.description: Identifier aliases from other ontologies. Populate every applicable field — multiple aliases per node is expected.

status.description: Anatomist-review state. Only 'reviewed' ships to the user-facing app.

source.description: Where this relation is asserted (UBERON, FMA, hand-curated, etc.).

version.description: Timestamped version (YYYY-MM-DD). Anatomy evolves; timestamps are clearer than semver for this domain. Every file in the anatomy data split (nodes.json, relations.json) carries the same version per coordinated edit.

nodes.description: Anatomy-graph nodes. Populated in nodes.json; empty array (or absent) in relations.json.

edges.description: Anatomy-graph typed edges. Populated in relations.json; empty array (or absent) in nodes.json.
